beats.activity: Wake up, make coffee and breakfast, check the weather and news.
beats.reasoning: Raj starts his day as a homebody with his usual routine—coffee and eggs on toast. The grey, drizzly forecast sets a low-key tone; he's unbothered by the rain and SR-520 closure since he knows to avoid the bridge during peak hours.

beats.activity: Drive to Kirkland via I-5 and alternate route, avoiding SR-520 eastbound closure.
beats.reasoning: SR-520 eastbound is closed 9am–noon for emergency repair; Raj leaves early enough to take I-5 south and loop around via I-90 westbound to Kirkland, avoiding the snarl entirely. Adds buffer time but keeps him stress-free.

beats.activity: Prep work and lunch service as chef de partie.
beats.reasoning: Raj settles into his station for the morning prep and lunch service. Steady work, nothing wild expected on a rainy Tuesday; he's in his element in the kitchen.

beats.activity: Lunch break; grab a meal in the restaurant or nearby.
beats.reasoning: Raj takes a break during the lull between lunch and dinner prep. He often grabs leftovers or a quiet meal in the back, decompressing and listening to the kitchen rhythm.

beats.activity: Afternoon prep and prep for dinner service.; Dinner service; plating, managing the station, ensuring quality.
beats.reasoning: Raj returns to his station for afternoon prep and mise en place, readying for the dinner rush. Rainy Tuesday means steady but not chaotic; he's focused and methodical.

beats.activity: Drive home from Kirkland via I-5, rain has cleared.
beats.reasoning: Service ends; Raj heads home after a full day. Rain has stopped by evening (cleared after 2pm), and he takes the straightforward I-5 north route back to Greenwood, no bridge drama.

beats.activity: Wind down at home: pour a homebrew, crack open a book, relax.
beats.reasoning: Raj decompresses with his favorite rituals—a homebrew and a book. He's a homebody at heart, and after a solid day of service, this quiet time is exactly what he needs to recharge.
Raj heads to bed after a long, focused day. Seven hours of sleep will restore him for tomorrow, and the overnight beat visibly spans the calendar boundary.

diary: Woke to grey skies and that persistent drizzle—the kind that makes you want to stay in bed with your coffee. Checked the news and saw the SR-520 closure would be a nightmare, so I mapped out I-5 instead. Good call. The drive wasn't too bad, just slower than usual with everyone else rerouting. Hit the restaurant by 8:20 and jumped straight into prep. Lunch service moved smoothly; we had a decent crowd despite the weather keeping people indoors. Grabbed a quick meal during my break—one of our specials, some roasted fish thing—and honestly, it hit different when you're exhausted. Afternoon flew by. Dinner service kept me sharp at my station; a few tricky plates came through but nothing we couldn't handle. By the time I clocked out, the rain had cleared. Drive home felt lighter somehow. Poured myself one of my homebrews, cracked open that book I've been meaning to start. Just needed the quiet.
thought_process: The weather and bridge closure shaped everything today, but in a weird way it focused me. I could've stressed about traffic, but I made the call early and it paid off. That's the thing about routine—you learn to adapt without overthinking it. Work demanded most of my energy; dinner service always does. I was in my zone, which meant the day flew. I think the exhaustion actually helped—less time in my head, more time just executing. By evening, when the rain cleared and I got home, I felt that familiar relief. Nothing dramatic happened, but there was this rhythm to it all that felt solid. The homebrew and book were exactly what I needed. Sometimes a Tuesday is just a Tuesday, but you find the small satisfactions where you can.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
